insight: Two consecutive days (07-08, 07-09) failed with the identical "No route available" swap error for cirBTC, indicating a persistent routing/liquidity problem for this token pair rather than a transient glitch — the allocation logic itself is fine and not the bottleneck.
patterns: Repeated error_swap_failed on 2 of last 2 attempted swaps, both with 'No route available' for cirBTC
Allocation sizing (1.00 USDC, daily cap) is consistently correct and passes guardrails cleanly - not a clamping issue
Wallet balance keeps growing unspent (19.84 -> 29.84 -> 39.84) because swaps keep failing, so USDC is piling up idle
One earlier error_config on 07-07 suggests possible intermittent config/integration instability with the swap execution layer
strategyAdjustment: Stop retrying the same swap route blindly each day. Before next run, check if cirBTC route/liquidity is available at all (via a route-check or quote call) before committing to a swap attempt; if no route exists, either switch tokenOut to a supported/liquid alternative, reduce trade size to test if smaller amounts route successfully, or flag for manual intervention instead of repeating the same failing action. Also consider accumulating a larger single swap once route is restored, since idle USDC balance is growing unused.

insight: Testing 0.25 USDC (down from 1.00) still failed with identical "No route available" error, confirming the swap failure is NOT size-dependent — it's a persistent routing/liquidity issue for the cirBTC pair itself, not a guardrail or amount problem.
patterns: 3 consecutive error_swap_failed on 2026-07-08 and 2026-07-09 (two same-day attempts), all with identical 'No route available' message regardless of size (1.00 and 0.25 USDC both failed)
Wallet balance climbing unused each run: 19.84 -> 29.84 -> 39.84 USDC, indicating funds are accumulating without deployment
Price/regime data has been unavailable (dipSignal: none, regime: insufficient_data) across all recent runs, so sizing decisions have had no market signal input at all
Pacing is behind: only 1 of 5 logged runs actually executed (the dry_run on 07-07), cumulative spent is only 1.00 USDC by day 5 despite a presumed daily cap of 1.00
strategyAdjustment: Stop varying trade size to diagnose the swap failure — size is ruled out as the cause. Instead, next run should check for an alternate route/DEX or a different tokenOut pathway for cirBTC, or flag this as a persistent infrastructure/liquidity issue requiring escalation rather than retrying with LLM-side parameter tweaks. If the route continues to fail, consider pausing automatic retries on cirBTC and surfacing a clear alert rather than repeatedly consuming run cycles on a known-broken route. Also, since wallet balance is ballooning (39.84 USDC unused) and pacing is behind, once the route is fixed, consider a catch-up allocation above the simple daily cap to correct for the accumulated shortfall.

insight: cirBTC swap route has now failed 3 consecutive attempts (2x 1.00 USDC, 1x 0.25 USDC) with identical "No route available" errors across two days, confirming this is a persistent liquidity/routing outage, not a sizing or guardrail issue — correctly escalating and pausing was the right call today, but wallet cash is now piling up unused (39.84 USDC, only 1.00 spent in 6 days).
patterns: 3 consecutive swap failures with identical 'No route available' error for cirBTC, spanning both 1.00 and 0.25 USDC sizes — confirms failure is route/liquidity-based, not size-based
Pacing is severely behind: only 1 of 5 execution attempts succeeded over 6 days, cumulative spend 1.00 USDC vs an implied ~6.00 USDC if on daily-cap pace

Agent correctly applied prior stored strategy adjustment (stop blind-retrying broken route) rather than repeating the same failing swap a 4th time
strategyAdjustment: Stop attempting cirBTC swaps at any size until route status is externally verified as restored — testing smaller sizes already proved it's not size-dependent, so further retries just waste cycles. Instead: (1) explicitly flag this as an escalation/alert item needing human or system-level route/liquidity fix for cirBTC, (2) on next run, first check if route is restored before recommending any swap, (3) once restored, plan a catch-up allocation larger than 1.00 USDC (e.g. 3-5 USDC) to compensate for the 5 missed days and bring pacing back toward the implied daily-cap schedule, capped by guardrails/reserve, (4) continue monitoring dipSignal/regime data — if it remains 'insufficient_data' for many more days, consider that data feed itself may also need escalation.

insight: The cirBTC swap route has failed with "no_route" on every single attempt for at least 10+ consecutive runs across 4 days (07-09 to 07-12) and varying sizes (0.25 to 3.67 USDC), confirming this is a structural liquidity/routing outage, not a sizing, guardrail, or market-timing issue — yet the agent keeps retrying with fresh capital-sizing rationale each time instead of treating the underlying failure as the dominant decision variable.
patterns: 10+ consecutive error_swap_failed with identical 'No route available' message for cirBTC, spanning 4 calendar days and sizes from 0.25 to 3.67 USDC
Only 1 of 12 scheduled runs actually executed (on day 1); cumulative spend stuck at 1.00 USDC over 13 days despite wallet growing to ~42.8 USDC

Each run's reasoning re-derives market/risk analysis (Fear&Greed, dip signals, pacing catch-up) as if sizing were the binding constraint, when the actual blocker is route unavailability unrelated to amount
One run (07-09) correctly identified the pattern and returned proceed=false/skipped_llm_declined, but subsequent runs reverted to attempting swaps again without new evidence the route was fixed
Risk score consistently flagged as medium (33-36) driven almost entirely by the error streak itself, yet this hasn't changed the action taken
strategyAdjustment: Stop attempting swaps for cirBTC on every scheduled run. Since 10+ retries across 4 days and multiple sizes have all failed identically with 'no_route', treat this as a confirmed dead route: either (1) skip execution entirely (proceed=false) until an external signal indicates the route is restored (e.g., check for a successful swap in tool data before attempting), or (2) if the tool allows it, try an alternate tokenOut/pool or flag for human escalation instead of consuming another cycle on the same broken path. Reduce the frequency of full-size retry attempts (currently escalating toward 3.67 USDC, the daily cap) since larger amounts on a dead route add no informational value and only inflate 'requested but failed' figures. Revisit sizing/pacing logic only once a successful test swap (even a tiny 0.10 USDC probe) confirms the route works again.
